text:
6:42 Each time I rewatch this video, this part hits me like a speedbump. One moment you are enjoying the simple matters of running outside when there's smoke outside or not going outside then run outside etc.

And the next moment you are reminded of your mortality. The inevitable absolute void of death that could strike at any moment. The epicentre of every living being's fears. The notion of nonexistence sending chills down whatever vessel you possess. The uncaring universe has no destiny, no fate nor meaning to any of it.
But at the same time, fearing it might make you ignore what you have right now. Nonexistence might be the end but the opposite is true for existence. Opportunities arise if you keep your eyes open for them. So I think it is fitting the next scene depicts something symbolizing a beginning; children crawling, crawling, crawling and crawling... 
And crawling...

Then some 2022 topical joke about covid i dunno.

text:
Unrelated but related to the video. Youtube has removed me entire 10 year old YTP playlist and gave me a strike on my channel, citing "hate speach". The appeal did not return any information beyond "no this is hate speech". It refused to showed me which video was the issue or removed the video itself and leave me alone. I did not make any YTP nor uploaded any video of my own in the playlist.

With this information at hand I plead with ya guys, keep your YTP playlist private and not just unlisted. Don't end up like me and have 10 years of memories nuked with no way to get it back beyond making a stir on twitter and pretending there is a chance.
author: @theking4562

text: I've heard about similar situations for those who've bookmarked even simpler things, like music.  The playlist creator gets a strike just because they made a playlist featuring one piece of content that got removed... That sounds absolutely nuts and I can't believe it's been happening.  Sorry that all of that was lost.
author: @cs188

text:
@cs188   @cs188  Thanks for the sympathy and related info. I'm going to see if I can make a support ticket and or ""communicate"" via Twitter. Sometime later this month when life is less hectic.

I believe what got me was some stipulations of not removing striker videos from the playlist, it's suppose to auto delete stuff when you reopen a playlist but I guess it doesn't do that until you manually reopen a playlist oneself. So all those taken down videos remain in the playlist I did not check in awhile and it all somehow cumulated in a bot removing my playlist and a 2nd bot denying me a way out + explanation.
